title: Chain-Road Dawn
note: Generated from Wilderland liberation Ch1 spine (canonical with story-spine.json).

frames.title: Cage Line
frames.body: Mist sits on the Chain-Road like damp wool soaked in livestock math. Iron cages rattle on ox-wains while overseers count numbers instead of names — and spit when the count comes up short. You are Nine-Mark: thrall, fighter, and cargo that punches back when the whip gets lazy.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Collar Echo
frames.body: Halbrecht Quill lights a match off a warrant nail and looks almost human. Freedom still feels like hunger wearing better boots. West is a rumor; east is a debt with a smile.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Warrant Weather
frames.body: Camp smoke writes temporary law across the pines. Tonight’s camp will argue theology with mosquitoes and memory. Quill packs the silence like powder — dry, useful, dangerous.

frames.title: Road Vermin Tax
frames.body: Three road vermin block a ford with a rope and a grin. Their leader — missing two fingers and all shame — offers “protection” for coin, a story, or a bruise. Quill’s hand rests near his bolt like punctuation.

choices.label: Pay the damn rope
choices.approach: Buy the ford; save steel for Vern.

choices.label: Scare them honest
choices.approach: Show the freemark and the crossbow’s opinion.

choices.label: Trade a Chain-Road story
choices.approach: Give them a tale sharp enough to sell twice.

frames.title: Quill's Ugly Joke
frames.body: At dusk Quill asks whether you want to be a freeman who hunts, or a hunter who happens to be free. The difference, he says, is who you apologize to when the smoke clears.

choices.label: Freeman who hunts
choices.approach: Keep the moral order: freedom first, warrants second.

choices.label: Hunter who is free
choices.approach: Admit the work shapes the man.

choices.label: Neither — Lyra’s man
choices.approach: Refuse Quill’s categories; keep the vow central.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ash Aside
frames.body: Steel rides light; the ledger in Quill’s coat does not. You catch yourself walking like cargo that learned a verb. Continue — soft roads make soft collars again.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Quill's Aside
frames.body: Lyra’s name sits behind your teeth like a loaded prayer. Quill drawls, “Paper freedom first. Real freedom files later.” Another frame turns like a dusty page with teeth.

frames.title: The Binder Arrives
frames.body: A lean man in travel-stained grey tips his hat to the overseer as if haggling over mule feed and mildly disappointing weather. Halbrecht Quill — warrant binder, chain-buyer, scholar of ugly laws — pays coin for your papers without looking away from your eyes. “Pretty muscle,” he tells the overseer. “Ugly price. I’ll take him.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Cane Wind
frames.body: Lyra’s name sits behind your teeth like a loaded prayer. Freedom still feels like hunger wearing better boots. Steel rests light; resolve does not.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Dust Mile
frames.body: Quill buys your freedom like a man purchasing a complicated knife. The next mile pretends to be empty and fails the audition. Night takes attendance; you answer as a freeman.

frames.title: Quill Names the Work
frames.body: “I buy thralls who can ruin the men who sold them,” Quill says, not unkind. “If that offends your poetry, walk. If it fits your teeth, load.” Ash loads.

frames.title: Vern's Joke
frames.body: A traveler repeats Vern’s favorite joke about unpaid tribute and burned kitchens. Nobody laughs. Quill tips the man a copper for the geography hidden in the cruelty.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Old Hunger
frames.body: Lyra’s name sits behind your teeth like a loaded prayer. Road vermin sell water and buy stories about numbered men. The cane smell thickens like a dare.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Freemark Thoughts
frames.body: Ash tastes copper on the wind and does not flinch. Quill says, “Pretty country. Ugly ownership.” Quill tips his hat at unfinished justice.

frames.title: Paper Freedom
frames.body: Quill slides a stamped sheet across the wagon board. Freedom on parchment, conditional as a hangman’s courtesy: hunt the guilty he names, keep steel sharp, ask no soft questions. “Say yes,” he offers, almost kindly, “or climb back into inventory. I don’t do poetry.”

choices.label: Take the warrant path
choices.approach: Accept conditional freedom; work for Quill.

choices.label: Bargain for a name first
choices.approach: Ask whose name this work is really for.

choices.label: Test the man's honesty
choices.approach: Watch for a twitch that says he buys thralls for worse work.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ledger Weather
frames.body: Quill hums a tune that never finishes the same way twice. A joke dies mid-laugh when Cade’s check appears on a sleeve. The next mile does not apologize.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Steel Practice
frames.body: Somebody buried a whip and marked the dirt with hope. Ash keeps counsel; Quill keeps a ledger of silences. Ash sets the pace like a man owed a name.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Night Watch
frames.body: A child’s chalk freemark on a fence post has already been scrubbed. Quill drawls, “Paper freedom first. Real freedom files later.” Another frame turns like a dusty page with teeth.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Iron Humor
frames.body: Ash tastes copper on the wind and does not flinch. Tracks braid, unbraid, and lie with professional cheer. Lyra’s sale date ticks quieter than a clock and louder than fear.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Collar Echo
frames.body: A hanged scarecrow wears Cade’s check like a joke that landed. Quill says, “Pretty country. Ugly ownership.” West is a rumor; east is a debt with a smile.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Warrant Weather
frames.body: Wagon ruts braid into a grammar only hunters read fluently. The road offers three bad choices and one worse delay. Dust settles on everything except the vow.

frames.title: Collar Off
frames.body: The overseer pries the collar with a hooked key that smells of rust, old sweat, and other people’s surrender. Skin burns where iron sat for seasons. Quill tosses the ring into roadside mud like trash that once owned a man — then grinds it under his heel, just to be thorough.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Hard Mercy
frames.body: Ash tastes copper on the wind and does not flinch. A road-vermin kid offers directions priced in half-truths. Quill files Lowhedge under “paid in blood or nearly.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Vermin Bargain
frames.body: The road offers a fork that is really a dare. Quill files every kindness under “possibly tactical.” Another frame turns like a dusty page with teeth.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ash Aside
frames.body: Ash tastes copper on the wind and does not flinch. Freedom still feels like hunger wearing better boots. Continue — soft roads make soft collars again.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Quill's Aside
frames.body: Ash tastes copper on the wind and does not flinch. Quill’s tea tastes like boiled warrants and patience. You walk because the alternative still smells like rust.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Cane Wind
frames.body: A hanged scarecrow wears Cade’s check like a joke that landed. Quill files every kindness under “possibly tactical.” You spit iron taste and keep the freemark facing out.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Dust Mile
frames.body: Quill’s hat brim cuts the sun into a usable blade. A road-vermin kid offers directions priced in half-truths. Night takes attendance; you answer as a freeman.

frames.title: First Mile Free
frames.body: Boots that never chose a direction now choose west, then south, at Quill’s nudge. Crows watch the cages dwindle and look personally offended. Freedom feels like hunger with better manners and a loaded crossbow for a chaperone.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Quiet Counsel
frames.body: Lyra’s name sits behind your teeth like a loaded prayer. Road vermin sell water and buy stories about numbered men. You walk on because stopping is how numbers return.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Road Hygiene
frames.body: A creek argues with stones like a tired magistrate on half pay. A road-vermin kid offers directions priced in half-truths. Lyra’s sale date ticks quieter than a clock and louder than fear.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Old Hunger
frames.body: Chain-Road mist sits on the world like damp wool and worse math. Ash refuses to romanticize the road; the road returns the favor. Night takes attendance; you answer as a freeman.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Freemark Thoughts
frames.body: Heat makes the freemark itch like unfinished business. Somewhere east, ink still invents people into inventory. The next mile does not apologize.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ledger Weather
frames.body: Overseer laughter carries farther than any hymn on this road. Dust becomes a third companion with poor hygiene and worse jokes. Quill files Lowhedge under “paid in blood or nearly.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Steel Practice
frames.body: Bootleather remembers every mile better than maps ever will. Quill’s tea tastes like boiled warrants and patience. Somewhere east, a ledger page waits to be rewritten.

frames.title: Orc Chainers
frames.body: Three chain-orcs peel from the pine edge, still smelling of the cages they sold this morning. They want their property returned with interest. Quill calmly loads a crossbow and drawls, “Show them the paperwork is yours — and that the warranty expired.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Night Watch
frames.body: Ash tastes copper on the wind and does not flinch. Quill files every kindness under “possibly tactical.” Steel rests light; resolve does not.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Iron Humor
frames.body: Chain-Road mist sits on the world like damp wool and worse math. A joke dies mid-laugh when Cade’s check appears on a sleeve. Quill packs the silence like powder — dry, useful, dangerous.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Collar Echo
frames.body: Rain finds the places where collars used to teach posture. A hanged poster of your face lies about the eyes — always the eyes. The cane smell thickens like a dare.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Warrant Weather
frames.body: Two riders tip hats too friendly for the hour. You practice not answering to a number when strangers call. The Wilderland watches and keeps its receipts.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Hard Mercy
frames.body: Quill buys your freedom like a man purchasing a complicated knife. Dust becomes a third companion with poor hygiene and worse jokes. The cane smell thickens like a dare.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Vermin Bargain
frames.body: Clouds drag shadows across cane-colored grass like slow warrants. Your wrist freemark itches whenever eyes linger too long. The cane smell thickens like a dare.

frames.title: Quill's Lesson
frames.body: “Steel answers collar-law better than ink,” Quill says, wiping ichor from the bolt like a clerk correcting a sum. He drills stance, breath, and the difference between killing for a warrant and killing for rage. “Rage is fine,” he adds. “Just don’t let it hold the map.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ash Aside
frames.body: Overseer laughter carries farther than any hymn on this road. Vern’s ledger scrap burns colder than any campfire comfort. You walk because the alternative still smells like rust.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Quill's Aside
frames.body: Clouds drag shadows across cane-colored grass like slow warrants. A hanged poster of your face lies about the eyes — always the eyes. Paper in your coat weighs less than iron and more than sleep.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Cane Wind
frames.body: Camp smoke writes temporary law across the pines. Quill’s crossbow speaks better law than half the magistrates east. Quill nods toward the darker horizon without ceremony.

frames.title: Campfire Question
frames.body: Night fire snaps. Quill boils bitter tea that tastes like boiled warrants and asks what you will do if Lyra is already broken past rescue. The question sits between you like a third traveler with muddy boots and no manners.

choices.label: I bring her home anyway
choices.approach: Refuse the premise that anyone is past saving.

choices.label: Then Candlemire pays in blood
choices.approach: If she is gone, Cade Mire still owns the debt.

choices.label: I will decide when I see her
choices.approach: Keep counsel until the yard is real.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Old Hunger
frames.body: A warrant nail still bleeds pine sap where paper used to hang. A thrall brand on a fence rail dares you to look away. Chain-Road shrinks behind you like a bad religion losing believers.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Freemark Thoughts
frames.body: Cage wheels still squeak in the memory of your spine. Quill mutters law under his breath like a protective curse. Quill nods toward the darker horizon without ceremony.

frames.title: Warrant One
frames.body: A farmstead burned for unpaid tribute; the killer rides with a brand that matches Cade’s outer seals like a signature on arson. Quill’s warrant names him Vern of Lowhedge. “Alive if possible,” Quill says. “Dead if the world insists on being itself.”

frames.title: Lowhedge Ruins
frames.body: Ash roofs. A child’s doll face-down in soot. Tracks lead into scrub where wargs like soft meat and softer excuses. Somewhere ahead, Vern laughs at a joke only hunters and monsters find funny.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Cane Wind
frames.body: Dust lifts off the track in pale sheets that lie for a living. Vern’s ledger scrap burns colder than any campfire comfort. Quill nods toward the darker horizon without ceremony.

frames.title: Dust Wargs
frames.body: Two dust-wargs burst from thorn. Their eyes reflect fire they did not start and do not regret. Quill covers the left; yours is the right throat — if you still remember what cages taught about hesitation.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ledger Weather
frames.body: A warrant nail still bleeds pine sap where paper used to hang. You trade a story for water and keep the ending private. West is a rumor; east is a debt with a smile.

frames.title: Vern Cornered
frames.body: Vern sits against a tree with a leg broken by his own horse — comedy the forest did not request. He begs for Cade’s protection and offers a torn ledger page with women’s names. Lyra’s sits three lines down, sold as “song-thrall, unbroken.” Vern smiles like that should buy him sunrise.

choices.label: Bind him for trial
choices.approach: Alive for Quill’s warrant and a clean conscience.

choices.label: End him for Lowhedge
choices.approach: Justice for ash roofs; take the page either way.

choices.label: Press for Candlemire routes
choices.approach: Pain or promise — get the eastern road correct.

frames.title: Name on Paper
frames.body: You say Lyra aloud until the vowels stop shaking and start sounding like a plan. Quill packs Vern’s routes — or what remains of them — and points toward a freeman town that stamps men as unpaid labor instead of numbered iron. Dawn smells like pine, powder, and unfinished debts.
